---
title: "dsci445termProject.rmd"
author: "Mark Gardner"
date: "11/9/2021"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(GGally)
library(ISLR)
library(class)
library(MASS)
library(dplyr)
# realData <- read_csv("train.csv")
realData <- read_csv("realEstate.csv")
head(realData)
colnames(realData)
re <- realData
# get rid of useless columns
re <- re[,c(-(1:3),-7,-12,-18,-27,-29,-(31:68))]

# remove observations with missing data
re <- na.omit(re)
ggpairs(re)
colnames(realData)
colnames(re)
realData <- re
colnames(realData)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

summary(realData)
names(realData)
realData$MSZoning
realData[MSZoning == 'A']
realData[ realData[[MSZoning]] == 'A', ]
realData[ realData[, "MSZoning"] == "RL", ]
groupByZoning <- realData %>% group_by(MSZoning)
countByZoning <- realData %>% count(MSZoning)
countByZoning




```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}

library(ISLR) ## data package
library(tidyverse) ## data manipulation
library(knitr) ## tables
p <- ggplot(data = realData, mapping = aes(x=TotRmsAbvGrd, y = SalePrice, color = MSZoning)) + 
  #geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
  geom_point(alpha=.3)+ 
  scale_x_log10()+
  scale_y_continuous()+
  facet_wrap(~MSZoning)

colnames(re)
p <- ggplot(data = realData, mapping = aes(x=TotRmsAbvGrd, y = SalePrice, color = MSZoning)) + 
  #geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
  geom_point(alpha=.3)+ 
  scale_x_log10()+
  scale_y_continuous()+
  facet_wrap(~MSZoning)
p
#CORRECT 
names(realData)
summary(realData)
library(tree)
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
summary(realData$SalePrice)
colnames(realData)

```

```{r}
# realData2 <- realData %>% 
#  mutate(high_sales = factor(ifelse(SalePrice > 214000, "high" , "low")))
# address and description; raw; don't include 
summary(realData)
colnames(realData2)
realData2 <- realData
realData2 <- re
#re$`Lot Size`
head(realData2$Price)
#is.numeric(realData2$Price)
# db <- gsub(",","",db)
#realData2$Price <- gsub("$", "", realData2$Price)
#realData3$Price <- gsub(",", "", realData2$Price)
realData2$Price <- gsub(",", "", realData2$Price)
separatedPrice <- realData2$Price
realData2$SqrFt <- gsub(",", "", realData2$SqrFt)
#str_replace(realData2$Price, "[$]", "")
#realData2$Price <- as.numeric((realData2$Price))
#realData2$Price <- substring(2, realData2$Price)
#summary(realData2$Price)
head(realData2$Price)
realData2$Price <- realData2$Price %>% 
  str_replace("[$]", "")
realData2$SqrFt <- realData2$SqrFt %>% 
  str_replace(" sqft", "")
#is.numeric(realData2$SqrFt)
#is.character(realData2$SqrFt)
realData2$SqrFt <- as.numeric(realData2$SqrFt)
# NAs introduced; how to create average of that missing value? 
# should long and lat be numeric? character? factor? 
# lotSize 
#hairdo %>% mutate(colour = ifelse(str_detect(hair, "Long."), "Blond", "Brunette"))
# realData2$lotSize
#realData2$newLotSize <- c()
#realData2CorrectLotSize <- realData2 %>% mutate(realData2$newLotSize, ifelse(str_detect(lotSize, "acres"), as.numeric(realData2$lotSize) * 43560, lotSize))

#realData2CorrectLotSize$
realData2$lotSize <- realData2$lotSize %>% 
  str_replace(" sqft", "") 
is.numeric(realData2$lotSize)
is.character(realData2$lotSize)
realData2$lotSize <- as.numeric(realData2$lotSize)


# from sam's 
# from sam's 
#inSqft <- rep(NA,length(realData2$lotSize))
for (i in 1:length(realData2$lotSize)) {
  if (grepl("sqft",realData2$lotSize[i],fixed=TRUE)) {
    #inSqft[i] <- TRUE
    # re$LotSize[i] <- re$LotSize[i]*(1/43560)
    
    realData2$lotSize[i] <- parse_number(realData2$lotSize[i])*(1/43560)
  } else {
    #inSqft[i] <- FALSE
   realData2$lotSize[i] <- parse_number(realData2$lotSize[i])
  }
}
# sam's 
#inSqft <- rep(NA,length(re$lotSize))
#for (i in 1:length(re$lotSize)) {
#  if (grepl("sqft",re$lotSize[i],fixed=TRUE)) {
#    inSqft[i] <- TRUE
##  } else {
#    inSqft[i] <- FALSE
#  }
#}
# 


realData2 <- realData2 %>% 
  mutate(high_sales = factor(ifelse(Price > 400000, "high" , "low"))) 
min(realData2$lotSize)
max(realData2$lotSize)

colnames(realData2)
summary(realData2$high_sales)
# realData2 <- subset(realData2, select = -c(SalePrice))
realData2 <- subset(realData2, select = -c(Price))
colnames(realData2)
#realData2 <- subset(realData2, select = -c(3SsnPorch))
#colnames(realData2)[70] <- "thirdSSNPorch"
#colnames(realData2)
#colnames(realData2)[44]
#colnames(realData2)[44] <- "firstFloorSQFT"
#colnames(realData2)[45]
#colnames(realData2)[45] <- "secondFloorSQFT"

#colnames(realData2)

```

```{r}

nrow(realData2)
ncol(realData2)
# naCols <- vector(length=81)
naCols <- vector(length=22)
# for (i in 1:81){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(realData2[,i]))}
for (i in 1:22){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(realData2[,i]))}
subset <- realData2[,which(naCols < 10)]
ncol(subset)
nrow(subset)
colnames(subset)
colnames(realData2)
summary(realData2)

colnames(realData2)[4] <- "SqrFt"
colnames(realData2)
colnames(realData2)[4]
colnames(realData2)[7] <- "lotSize"
colnames(realData2)[7]
colnames(realData2)[10] <- "yearBuilt"
colnames(realData2)[10]
colnames(realData2)[11] <- "pricePerSqFoot"
colnames(realData2)[11]
colnames(realData2)[12] <- "lastSoldYear"
colnames(realData2)[12]
colnames(realData2)[13] <- "lastSoldFor"
colnames(realData2)[13]
colnames(realData2)[14] <- "lastTaxAssessment"
colnames(realData2)[14]
colnames(realData2)[15] <- "lastTaxYear"
colnames(realData2)[15]
colnames(realData2)[20] <- "addressFull"
colnames(realData2)[12]
colnames(realData2)[21] <- "dayOnTrulia"
colnames(realData2)[21]

```

```{r}

tree_fit <- tree(high_sales ~ ., data = subset)
summary(tree_fit)
plot(tree_fit)
text(tree_fit)

n <- nrow(subset)
n
# 2nd try
n <- floor(.8 * nrow(subset))
n
train_ind <- sample(seq_len(nrow(subset)),  size = n)
train_subset <- subset[train_ind,]
nrow(train_subset)
test_subset <- subset[-train_ind,]
nrow(test_subset)
class(test_subset)
#test <- -train_ind
length(test)
# subset2
train_subset <- subset2[train_ind,]
nrow(train_subset)
ncol(train_subset)
ncol(test_subset)

test_subset <- subset2[-train_ind,]
nrow(test_subset)
ncol(test_subset)
#
naCols <- vector(length=14)
for (i in 1:14){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(train_subset[,i]))}
train_subset_2 <- train_subset[,which(naCols < 10)]

naCols <- vector(length=14)
for (i in 1:14){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(test_subset[,i]))}
test_subset_2 <- test_subset[,which(naCols < 10)]

####naCols <- vector(length=22)
# for (i in 1:81){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(realData2[,i]))}
####for (i in 1:22){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(realData2[,i]))}
####subset <- realData2[,which(naCols < 10)]
#train <- sample(seq_len(n), 6500)
#length(train)
#test <- -train
#length(test)
#train_subset <- subset[train,]
tree_fit_final <- tree(high_sales ~ ., train_subset)
tree_fit_final <- tree(high_sales ~ ., train_subset_2)
summary(tree_fit_final)

#confusion <- table(pred = predict(tree_fit_final, subset[test,], type = "class"), true = subset[test,]$high_sales)
confusion <- table(pred = predict(tree_fit_final, subset2[test,], type = "class"), true = subset2[test,]$high_sales)

(confusion[1, 2] + confusion[2, 1])/sum(confusion)

```


```{r}

# removing unneeded stuff; creating as.factor and as.numeric. 
names(subset)
names(subset2)
subset2 <- subset(subset, select = -c(Title))
subset2 <- subset(subset, select = -c(Title, Description, Address, addressFull, City, State, Zipcode, Style)) #ADD STYLE BACK 
names(subset2)
subset2$lotSize
subset2$lastTaxAssessment <- parse_number(subset2$lastTaxAssessment)
# break this into two level factor.
subset2$Style <- as.factor(subset2$Style)
subset2$lotSize <- parse_number(subset2$lotSize)
subset2$pricePerSqFoot <- parse_number(subset2$pricePerSqFoot)
subset2$lastSoldFor <- parse_number(subset2$lastSoldFor)
subset2$City <- as.factor(subset2$City)
subset2$State <- as.factor(subset2$State)
subset2$Zipcode <- as.factor(subset2$Zipcode)

tree_cv <- cv.tree(tree_fit_final, FUN = prune.misclass)
tree_cv$size # What does this mean? 
prune_fit <- prune.misclass(tree_fit, k = tree_cv$k[which.min(tree_cv$dev)])
library(glmnet)
# One error we see here: low predictive power for high valuation; too few observations. Monte carlo ? 
#confusion_prune <- table(pred = predict(prune_fit, subset[test,], type = "class"), true = subset[test,]$high_sales)

confusion_prune <- table(pred = predict(tree_fit_final, test_subset, type = "class"), true = test_subset$high_sales)
confusion_prune

(confusion_prune[1, 2] + confusion_prune[2, 1])/sum(confusion_prune)

# This one performs better by about .02 . 
library(randomForest) 

# check all variables; make char to int or decimal where nececssary. ; make all char to factor if appropriate. 
summary(subset)

bag_fit <- randomForest(high_sales ~ ., data = train_subset, mtry = ncol(train_subset) - 1, importance = TRUE)

data.frame(bag_fit$importance )%>%
  mutate(variable = rownames(bag_fit$importance)) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = variable[order(MeanDecreaseGini)])) %>% ## trick to plot variable by descending Gini
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(aes(MeanDecreaseGini, variable))
  # latitute = california or the north? 
```

```{r}
confusion_bag <- table(pred = predict(bag_fit, test_subset_2, type = "class"), true = test_subset_2$high_sales)
# Some weird, unknown or uninitialized column error 
(confusion_bag[1, 2] + confusion_bag[2, 1])/sum(confusion_bag)

rf_fit <- randomForest(high_sales ~ ., data = train_subset,  mtry = sqrt(ncol(train_subset) - 1), importance = TRUE)

rf_fit <- randomForest(high_sales ~ ., data = trn_df, mtry = sqrt(ncol(trn_df) - 1), importance = TRUE)

data.frame(rf_fit$importance)%>%
  mutate(variable = rownames(rf_fit$importance)) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = variable[order(MeanDecreaseGini)])) %>% ## trick to plot variable by descending Gini
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(aes(MeanDecreaseGini, variable)) 

train_subset_2$
test_subset_2$

# PROBLEM: get predicted high/low and prices for that subset; not original separated prices 
# separatedPrice
summary(bag_fit)
bag_fit$predicted

bag_fit$terms
bag_fit$forest
```

```{r}
confusion_rf <- table(pred = predict(rf_fit, test_subset_2, type = "class"), true = test_subset_2$high_sales)

(confusion_rf[1, 2] + confusion_rf[2, 1])/sum(confusion_rf)
```

```{r}
confusion_rf
```
```{r}
c(confusion_rf[2, 1], confusion_rf[1, 2])/colSums(confusion_rf)
```
```{r}
rf_fit$confusion

```

```{r}
#boosting 
library(gbm) ## boosting package
subset2$high_sales
subset_boost$high_sales <- subset2 %>% 
  mutate(subset2, high_sales = ifelse(high_sales = "high", 1, 0))
  
subset_2_boost$high_sales

subset_2_boost <- subset2 %>%
  mutate(high_sales = 2 - as.numeric(high_sales))

train_subset_2_boost <- train_subset_2 %>%
  mutate(high_sales = 2 - as.numeric(high_sales))

boost_fit <- gbm(high_sales ~ ., train_subset_2_boost, n.trees = 5000, shrinkage = 0.1, interaction.depth = 2, distribution = "bernoulli")

confusion_boost <- table(pred = predict(boost_fit, train_subset_2, type = "response") > 0.5, true = train_subset_2$high_sales)

(confusion_boost[1, 2] + confusion_boost[2, 1])/sum(confusion_boost)

confusion_boost

c(confusion_boost[2, 1], confusion_boost[1, 2])/colSums(confusion_boost)

```

```{r}


realData[70]
is.data.frame(realData2)

names(realData2[70]) <- "thirdSSNPorch"
typeof(realData2)
df <- as.data.frame(realData2)
df[70]           
typeof(df)
realData2 <- realData2 %>% 
  rename(3SsnPorch = thirdSSNPorch)

# CORRECT 

df <- Carseats
df <- df %>%
  mutate(high_sales = factor(ifelse(Sales > 8, "high", "low"))) %>%
  select(-Sales)

tree_fit <- tree(high_sales ~ ., realData2)
```

```{r}
lm <- lm(SalePrice ~ Id + MSSubClass + MSZoning , data = realData)
lm <- lm(SalePrice ~  ., data = realData[,values_count > 1])

summary(lm)
summary(realData)
(l <- sapply(realData, function(x) is.factor(x)))
m <- realData[, l]
m
ifelse(n <- sapply(m, function(x) length(levels(x))) == 1, "DROP", "NODROP")
n

values_count <- sapply(lapply(realData, unique), length)

names(realData)

p0 <- ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x=carat, y = price, color = color)) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
  #geom_point(alpha=.5)
# p


p <- ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x=carat, y = price, color = color)) + 
  #geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
  geom_point(alpha=.1)
p0 <- ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x=carat, y = price, color = color)) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
  #geom_point(alpha=.5)
# p

ggpairs(realData)
head(realData)
cols = colnames(realData)
for (col in cols){
  if(is.factor(realData[[col]])){
    cat(col, ' has ', length(levels(realData[[col]])), '\n')
  }
}

summary(realData)

```

## Including Plots 

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
